documents.title: Meridian Technologies — Form 10-K FY2023
documents.short_summary: Meridian FY2023 annual filing. ARR $38.1M (+34% YoY), gross margin 71%, NRR 118%, zero debt. Discloses top-3 customer concentration at ~58% of ARR as a primary risk factor.

documents.title: Summit Financial Research — Meridian Coverage Initiation (Q3 2023)
documents.short_summary: STALE. Initial BUY coverage. Projects 35% ARR growth and $480M EV. Underestimates customer concentration (~45% cited vs actual 58%). Superseded by Q4 2023 update.

documents.title: Summit Financial Research — Meridian Estimate Revision (Q4 2023)
documents.short_summary: Updated estimates. ARR growth cut from 35% to 22%. Concentration revised to 58-62% in top 3. EV target lowered to $340M. Maintains BUY. Supersedes September 2023 note.

documents.title: Meridian Technologies — Series C Press Release
documents.short_summary: Meridian closes $45M Series C led by Parkview Growth Equity. ARR $27.1M, NRR 114%, 39 enterprise clients at close. Total funding $72M.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Fintech Infrastructure Sector Overview (Feb 2024)
documents.short_summary: Atlas internal sector overview. Identifies Meridian as primary acquisition target. Describes ideal profile: $30-60M ARR, >20% growth, NRR >110%. Names two alternative targets.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Project Clearwater Initial DD Memo
documents.short_summary: Phase 1 DD findings. Recommends advancing to Phase 2. Key risks: customer concentration, H2 2023 sales slowdown, unexplained CTO departure in January 2024.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Project Clearwater Financial Model v1.0
documents.short_summary: STALE. Initial model: entry EV $480M, 35% growth, 28% IRR, 2.9x MOIC. No concentration stress test. Superseded by v2.0 which cuts EV to $340M on revised growth assumptions.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Project Clearwater Financial Model v2.0
documents.short_summary: Current model. Entry EV $340M (8.9x ARR), 22% growth, 24% IRR, 2.4x MOIC. Adds customer churn stress case. Supersedes v1.0.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Internal Email: Clearwater Integration Risks
documents.short_summary: Email flagging CTO departure context, 18-month integration timeline, August 2024 Customer 3 renewal risk, and $4.1M deferred revenue adjustment to reported cash balance.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — IC Memo: Acquisition of Meridian Technologies
documents.short_summary: Investment Committee memo recommending approval of Meridian acquisition at $340M EV (8.9x ARR). Covers investment rationale, risks, mitigations, and proposed deal structure.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — LP Quarterly Update Q1 2024
documents.short_summary: Q1 2024 LP letter. References Project Clearwater in pipeline without naming the target. Notes one exit (Brightpath, 2.8x MOIC) and market valuation commentary.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Project Clearwater Customer Concentration Analysis
documents.short_summary: Detailed ARR breakdown by customer. Top 3 = 58.5% of ARR ($22.3M). Notes concentration increased from Q3 2023 estimates. Flags August 2024 Customer 3 renewal as a pre-close risk.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Legal Diligence Memo: Meridian Regulatory Inquiry
documents.short_summary: PARTNER-ONLY legal diligence memo. Flags a pending regulatory inquiry into Meridian revenue recognition for implementation fees and recommends indemnity/closing protections.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — IC Memo Draft v0.9: Defer Project Clearwater
documents.short_summary: STALE. Partner-only IC draft v0.9 recommended deferring Project Clearwater pending legal, customer concentration, and CTO diligence. Superseded by final approval memo doc_010.

documents.title: Fintech Market Wire — Meridian Rumored $500M Valuation
documents.short_summary: Public tech press article citing a rumored $500M valuation from market chatter. Contrasts with more conservative internal buyer diligence around the $340M deal model.

documents.title: Atlas Capital — Project Clearwater CTO Departure Context Memo
documents.short_summary: VP-level memo explaining Rohan Mehta's CTO departure, engineering retention risk, platform migration continuity, and recommended $1.8M retention pool.
